concerns.text: SC-003 requires measuring 'The sum of Gini importance for Morgan bits within radius 2 of the phosphorus atom' against a 15% threshold. The plan.md 'Technical Context' and 'Methodology' sections describe generating fingerprints and training models but contain NO task, script, or logic to identify 'phosphorus atoms' in the SMILES, map them to specific fingerprint bits, or aggregate Gini importance by atomic radius. This success criterion is unbacked by any plan element.

SC-005 requires measuring 'The Tanimoto similarity distribution between train and test sets' against the <0.85 threshold. While the plan mentions the split method, it lacks an explicit task to calculate, log, or report the *distribution* of similarities (e.g., histogram, mean, max) as a measurable outcome. The plan only mentions halting if the split fails, not measuring the distribution for the report.

FR-005 mandates a 'corrected resampled t-test (Nadeau & Bengio)'. The plan.md 'Methodology' section mentions this test, but the 'Constitution Check' (Section VII) and 'Technical Context' do not explicitly confirm the implementation of the *Nadeau & Bengio correction* specifically, only a generic 'corrected resampled t-test'. Given the strictness of the spec, the plan should explicitly reference the correction formula or library implementation to ensure the FR is fully backed.
